aesthetic: Warm minimal (Japanese stationery meets premium learning)

typography.korean_font_recommendation: Pretendard or System (Apple SD Gothic Neo/Noto Sans KR)

home_daily_path.description: Header with streak counter (terracotta flame icon). A massive, inviting 'Daily Path' card occupying the top half of the screen. Inside: progress bar, 'Learn 5 New' + 'Quiz' steps. Below: horizontal scroll of levels (8급 to 3급).

character_browsing_carousel.description: Full-screen horizontal paging ScrollView. Each character is a single screen-height card (minus header/nav). Clean, vast negative space. Massive Hanja centered. Swipe to move.

character_detail.description: Split view. Top 40%: The Hanja rendering (WebView with HanziWriter), soft #F7F3EE background. Bottom 60%: White card overlay sliding up (with 24px border radius on top corners), containing reading (음/훈), English meaning, radical, and the 'Word-First' list.
character_detail.word_first_list: Words displayed as thick pill-shaped rows (#F0E8DD background) with the target Hanja highlighted in #C97A3A.

quiz_cards.description: Flashcard metaphor. Large centered question. 3-4 massive answer buttons stacked vertically. Tapping an answer triggers haptic feedback and turns the button #5C8065 (Success) or #B3504B (Error).

navigation.style: Translucent white or solid white with top border #E8E2D9. No text labels, just clean icons, or very tiny text.

micro_interactions.entrance: Cards fade and slide up slightly (Y: 20 -> 0) on mount.
micro_interactions.feedback: Use expo-haptics for all correct/incorrect quiz answers and completing a daily path.
micro_interactions.swipe: Spring-based physics for character carousel swiping.

testing_and_accessibility.data_testid_rule: ALL interactive components, buttons, inputs, and major views MUST have a 'data-testid' attribute (e.g., 'home-daily-start-button', 'quiz-answer-option-1', 'nav-tab-search').
testing_and_accessibility.contrast: Ensure text over the terracotta accent is pure white for APCA readability.

general_instructions.styling: Use standard React Native StyleSheet unless a specific library like NativeWind is set up. Do not use generic web CSS.
